artifacts.role: typed shell metadata for the grouped WP_Http handle_redirects adapter
typed extern bridge to the generated Haxe redirect orchestration helper
grouped WP_Http helper compiler manifest proving fifteen adapter cases
WPHX-312.59 oracle/candidate manifest using the compiler-emitted original-path public shell
behavior receipt for the retired temporary shell candidate

claims: WPHX PHP emits the original-path WP_Http::handle_redirects public static adapter in the grouped class-wp-http.php shell.
The WPHX-312.59 candidate no longer copies and patches a PHP method body in its runner.

PHP still owns native Location header array handling, redirect budget mutation, WP_Error construction, WP_Http_Cookie::test, self::make_absolute_url, and wp_remote_request dispatch while Haxe owns bounded redirect short-circuit, too-many-redirects, and POST 302/303-to-GET decisions.
non_claims: This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership, live redirect following, Requests network I/O, DNS/proxy/TLS behavior, installed distribution behavior, or generated WP_Http_Cookie ownership.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
